entities.name: Anzu Robotics
entities.parent_entity: XTI Drones Holdings (subsidiary of XTI Aerospace, NASDAQ: XTIA)
entities.holding_company: The Origin Group AZ, Inc. (Delaware corporation, pre-acquisition)
entities.headquarters: 6500 River Place Blvd, Building 7, Suite 2, Austin, TX
entities.license_details: Licensed specs and SDK from DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. DJI receives per-unit payment. Warnas told NYT arrangement was essentially DJI's idea. DJI confirmed in Aug 2024 blog: standard and legal arrangement.
entities.anzu_compliance_statement: Explicitly stated: Not Blue UAS, not NDAA compliant, has Chinese components
entities.china_component_detail: ~50% from China. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset (proprietary). Thermal sensor from China. DJI batteries backward-compatible.
entities.data_hosting_provider: Aloft Technologies (Syracuse, NY — ISO 27001 / SOC 2 Type II)

security_architecture.firmware_signing: DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control
security_architecture.sdk: DJI SDK — Aloft app built on it
security_architecture.key_chipset: DJI P1 OcuSync ASIC — cannot be independently sourced
security_architecture.pentest: White Knight Labs May 2024 — data to Aloft only, Chinese domain refs remediated

fcc_filings.initial_disclosure: Did NOT disclose DJI relationship — disclosed only after researchers went public

security_researchers.name: [REDACTED — see authenticated portal]
security_researchers.contribution: Hardware teardowns — identical PCBs to DJI Mavic 3

security_researchers.name: [REDACTED — see authenticated portal]
security_researchers.contribution: Firmware analysis, documented 9+ suspected DJI shell companies

misleading_origin_claims: Your solution is delivered by your neighbors
voluntary_disclosures: Stated not Blue UAS and not NDAA compliant
Said 'If NDAA compliance is required we may not be the right product'

legal_actions.court: District Court of Collin County, Texas
legal_actions.case_context: Second of four CCP-aligned lawsuits that week (TP-Link, Lorex, Temu)
legal_actions.core_allegation: DJI drones in all but name — identical hardware, firmware, software, only green paint and American label
legal_actions.key_technical_claim: DJI retains root cryptographic keys — technical capability to access/modify/extract data
legal_actions.status: pending — no public response from Anzu as of April 2026
legal_actions.other_states: No other state AG has filed similar action

congressional_scrutiny.key_conclusion: Raptor T is essentially a DJI Mavic 3 painted green

congressional_scrutiny.action: Requested national security review of Chinese drone manufacturers

defenders: sUAS News: 'We are on team Anzu'
Industry: Warnas was more transparent than most
Some view ban effort as protectionism for Skydio/BRINC
critics: Researchers: transparency about licensing != transparency about DJI root keys
Warnas 'I like to be unaware' undermines independence claims

timeline.event: Warnas YouTube: DJI execs asked 'what can we do?'
Researchers publish teardown — identical PCBs, DJI crypto keys
DJI publishes 'Get The Facts' confirming license
Little Rock PD purchases 6 Raptor T
NDAA Section 1709 / FCC Covered List take effect
Texas AG lawsuit filed — 7 DTPA counts

entities.notes: Canonical gray zone case. Warnas was more transparent than Cogito/SkyRover but the gap between 'we licensed DJI tech' and 'DJI retains root crypto keys' is the core issue. NDAA Section 1709 explicitly covers licensing agreements — arguably written for this exact scenario. Randall is known in the industry and was upfront about many things — the community is divided on whether he was a bad actor or just caught in a regulatory vise.
flags_for_deeper_research: Full list of government buyers beyond 3 documented
Texas lawsuit status — has Anzu responded?
Warnas formal responses to Committee's 12 questions

latest_developments.tx_lawsuit_status: Filed Feb 18, 2026 in Collin County TX. 7 counts DTPA. No Anzu response filed as of April 4 — 45+ days frozen. TX seeking injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K/violation, and order requiring Anzu to disclose Raptor as rebranded DJI.
latest_developments.product_status: Entire Raptor product line discontinued Feb 10, 2026. Zero current products. No successor timeline disclosed.
latest_developments.part_of_broader_campaign: TX AG Paxton filed 4 CCP-linked lawsuits in one week: TP-Link, Anzu, Lorex, Temu. Coordinated enforcement strategy.
latest_developments.risk_assessment: CRITICAL. Zero products, active litigation, no response filed, parent company burning cash. Existential risk if TX ruling goes against Anzu — precedent for all gray zone entities.

flags.signal: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd.)

Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. Board part numbers match. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI RC Pro with identical firmware. Camera module, gimbal, propulsion all DJI-sourced. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset present — proprietary ASIC that cannot be independently sourced.

FCC filing reveals adversary components: Anzu did NOT disclose DJI relationship in initial FCC filings (Dec 2023). Only disclosed after security researchers publicly revealed connection in mid-2024. House Committee specifically cited this omission.
50% of components from adversary nations — partial pass-through

Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

SDK from adversary manufacturer embedded: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

SDK contacts adversary endpoints: White Knight Labs pentest confirmed data goes exclusively to Aloft servers — but found references to Chinese domains within system (remediated by Aloft). Texas AG alleges Aloft itself is a DJI-integrated platform. GitHub repo documented firmware accessible on DJI CDN.
Markets to government/public safety without Blue UAS clearance
Misleading origin claim: "American owned and operated"
Misleading origin claim: "Your solution is delivered by your neighbors"
Misleading origin claim: "An entirely American-owned company"
Product discontinued due to adversary component shortage

entities.name: Cogito Tech / SPECTA
entities.license_details: NO acknowledgment of DJI. Identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same P1 chipset, RF emission reports differ only by date/logo removal, firmware forked from DJI with branding stripped.

congressional_scrutiny.action: Named alongside Anzu in letter to Commerce Secretary

timeline.event: Specta Air appears — identified as DJI Air 3 clone
Named in congressional letter to Commerce

entities.notes: Less transparent than Anzu — zero DJI acknowledgment, no cybersecurity disclosure. Targets consumer market. Establishes the white-label playbook others replicate.

latest_developments.fcc_status: Subject to FCC Covered List as of Dec 22, 2025. No new models can receive FCC authorization. FCC has retroactive revocation authority since Oct 2025 vote.
latest_developments.shell_company_exposure: Identified by Konrad Iturbe's OcuSync fingerprinting bot. FCC filings under COGITO TECH COMPANY LIMITED (Hong Kong). Products: Specta Air (DJI Air 3 clone), Specta Mini.
latest_developments.market_status: Existing inventory may still be sold at retail. No pathway to new products under current FCC rules.
latest_developments.risk_assessment: HIGH. Smaller entity than Anzu/SkyRover. No US corporate presence. No litigation but fully exposed to FCC enforcement.

flags.signal: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (undisclosed — no licensing acknowledged)

Teardown confirms adversary hardware: Specta Air has identical PCBs to DJI Air 3. Same DJI P1 chipset. RF emission reports differ only by date and logo removal. Firmware forked from DJI with branding stripped. No independent technology whatsoever.
95% of components sourced from adversary nations
Misleading origin claim: "No country of origin disclosed"
Misleading origin claim: "No DJI acknowledgment"
Manufacturing in adversary nation: china (presumed)

entities.name: SkyRover / Knowact
entities.legal_name: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co., Ltd.
entities.license_details: App connects directly to DJI cloud. Identical encryption keys. DJI login credentials work. Zero corporate transparency. Layered structure for brand rotation if banned.

shell_company_network.pattern: FCC apps — Spatial Hover docs contained DJI logos

timeline.event: SkyRover X1 appears — DJI Mini 4 Pro clone identified
DroneXL exposure: DJI infrastructure links confirmed

entities.notes: Next-gen gray zone entity — emerged after Anzu drew scrutiny. Zero transparency, direct DJI cloud, layered corporate structure for resilience. 9+ suspected DJI shells documented.
flags_for_deeper_research: FCC action on shell company apps

latest_developments.us_manufacturing_claim: March 2026: SkyRover announced 5-year plan to 'explore US-based manufacturing and a localized supply chain.' Year 1: maintain FCC compliance. Years 2-3: build US team. Year 5: explore manufacturing. Key word: 'explore' — no commitment.
latest_developments.fcc_status: Subject to FCC Covered List. Existing S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory still available at retail. No new models can be authorized. Shell company filings identified: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd (Shenzhen), Skyhigh Tech LLC.
latest_developments.shell_network: 9+ entities identified by Konrad Iturbe OcuSync bot. FCC filings show: Knowact (drone bodies), Skyhigh Tech LLC (controllers). DJI logos visible in FCC antenna diagrams. WaveGo Tech filing included Knowact documentation.
latest_developments.corporate_opacity: SkyRover website contains no parent company, headquarters, or country of origin info. Only marketing language. SZ Knowact registered in Nanshan District, Shenzhen — same district as DJI HQ.
latest_developments.risk_assessment: CRITICAL (0.850). Most active remaining gray zone entity. Still selling product. Vague US manufacturing promise is years away. Corporate structure deliberately opaque.

flags.signal: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (undisclosed)
100% of components sourced from adversary nations

Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJI cloud infrastructure. Jon Sawyer found identical encryption keys. Users can log in using existing DJI credentials. This is the most direct DJI connection of any white-label — no Aloft-style intermediary.

SDK from adversary manufacturer embedded: SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJI cloud infrastructure. Jon Sawyer found identical encryption keys. Users can log in using existing DJI credentials. This is the most direct DJI connection of any white-label — no Aloft-style intermediary.
OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure
Data hosted in adversary nation: china (dji cloud)
Misleading origin claim: "No ownership disclosed"
Misleading origin claim: "No HQ disclosed"
Misleading origin claim: "No country disclosed"
Misleading origin claim: "Zero transparency"
Manufacturing in adversary nation: china

entities.name: Autel Robotics
entities.legal_name: Autel Robotics Co., Ltd. / Autel Robotics USA LLC
entities.license_details: Own technology — but openly Chinese-owned. Founded by Li Hongjing. Chinese govt 'Little Giant' designation. CITIC Corp (Ministry of Finance) among seed investors. Chinese website markets to 'public security, army, national defense, armed police.'
misleading_origin_claims: US subsidiary in Bothell creates impression of American company

regulatory_actions.action: Commerce Entity List — shipping controlled items to Russia
DoD Section 1260H Chinese Military Companies list

congressional_scrutiny.note: Named in NDAA, Entity List, Section 1260H

timeline.event: Warnas briefly CEO of NA subsidiary

entities.notes: Different gray zone position — openly Chinese but marketed as DJI alternative to US law enforcement. Own IP unlike Anzu/Cogito/SkyRover. Warnas's 9-week Autel stint connects these entities.

latest_developments.fcc_status: Named in Section 1709(a)(1) FY2025 NDAA alongside DJI. Added to FCC Covered List Dec 22, 2025. FCC revoked certain pre-ban authorizations in Jan 2026. Seeking reconsideration at FCC level.
latest_developments.section_1709_restrictions: Unlike generic foreign UAS ban, Autel's Section 1709 restrictions are ENTITY-SPECIFIC and extend to: any US-based production, joint ventures, technology sharing/licensing agreements. Cannot circumvent by onshoring.
latest_developments.market_impact: Existing authorized models remain legal to sell and operate. No new models can be authorized. DJI and Autel both filed for reconsideration; FCC placed on public notice.
latest_developments.conditional_approvals: 4 conditional approvals granted to other companies (not Autel/DJI) valid until Dec 31, 2026. FCC moving toward granular model-by-model review system. Blue UAS and Buy American exemptions in place until Jan 1, 2027.
latest_developments.risk_assessment: HIGH. Larger and more established than other gray zone entities. Has real products and real customers. But Section 1709 is uniquely restrictive — no pathway to US market even with onshoring.

dji_fcc_litigation.case: DJI v. FCC, Case 26-1029, Ninth Circuit
dji_fcc_litigation.status_april_2026: FCC moved to dismiss on ripeness grounds (bureau-level action, not final Commission order). Motion pending. DJI also filed motion for reconsideration at FCC.
dji_fcc_litigation.dji_arguments: FCC exceeded statutory authority, failed required procedures, violated Fifth Amendment. Never identified specific threat. No due process.
dji_fcc_litigation.timeline: Short-term: Gov response, possible preliminary injunction. Medium (6-18mo): Oral arguments. Long (1-3yr): Final resolution or trade negotiation settlement.
dji_fcc_litigation.fcc_exemptions: Jan 7 2026: exemptions for Blue UAS and 65% domestic content (expires Jan 1, 2027). Jan 8: Commerce Dept withdrew separate drone restrictions. March 2026: 4 conditional approvals granted to non-DJI/Autel companies.
dji_fcc_litigation.firmware_cliff: Jan 1, 2027: firmware waiver expires, Blue UAS exemption expires, conditional approvals expire. Triple deadline.

fcc_covered_list_status.scope: ALL foreign-produced UAS and UAS critical components

state_enforcement.texas: 4 CCP-linked lawsuits filed Feb 2026 (TP-Link, Anzu, Lorex, Temu). Gov Abbott expanded Prohibited Technologies List to include DJI, TP-Link.
state_enforcement.florida: Chinese drone ban enforced for state agencies.

metadata.enrichment_sources: Web search April 4, 2026. TX AG filings, FCC dockets, Ninth Circuit filings, SEC data, OSINT trackers, legal analyses.
metadata.note: risk_scores.json has been merged into this file. entities.json is the single source of truth for gray zone entity data.
